metadata.description: Community-maintained MLB National League injury database

injuries.status: Active — returned May 23, 2025 after ~1 year rehab; second hamstring issue this season
injuries.details: Tore left ACL near end of May 2024 while running the bases against the Pittsburgh Pirates — his second major knee injury (tore right ACL in July 2021). Underwent arthroscopic surgery on June 4, 2024 with Dr. Neal ElAttrache. By February 2025, reported at 90-95% in running and cutting with no restrictions. Expected to miss the first month of the 2025 season. Braves GM Alex Anthopoulos: 'We think getting him back is going to be big.' Finally returned to the Braves lineup on May 23, 2025, activated off the IL ahead of a game in Miami.
injuries.previous_injury_note: Previously tore right ACL on July 10, 2021; returned late April 2022 but dealt with lingering discomfort all season.
injuries.rehab_note: Rehabbed in Los Angeles for most of the 2024-2025 offseason. Braves management cautious about his return, noting he could need a Minor League rehab stint once cleared for cutting and outfield play.

injuries.status: Rehabbing — may not return until after All-Star break
injuries.details: Placed on 10-day IL with Grade 1 left hamstring strain after exiting Saturday's 9-1 win over the Rockies at Coors Field (June 10, 2025). This is his second hamstring issue of the 2025 season. Braves manager Walt Weiss stated Acuña is 'a long way' from returning as of late June 2025. Did not accompany the Braves on a road trip running through June 28. Expected to miss significant time with the possibility of not returning until after the All-Star break. May miss time until after the All-Star break due to the Grade 1 hamstring strain.

injuries.status: Recovered — lingering performance effects resolved by September
injuries.details: An undiagnosed stomach illness caused Betts to lose approximately 20 pounds in two weeks during spring 2025. He described feeling like his body was 'eating itself.' Missed the season-opening Tokyo Series in Japan. Returned on March 26, 2025 at ~157 lbs instead of his customary ~175 lbs. After a strong first 15 games (.304/.400/.554 with 4 HRs), he struggled to a .637 OPS over his next ~91 games due to both physical and mental effects of the illness. By August, he was hitting .330/.405/.454 since Aug. 5 and began to rediscover his form. The mental toll was significant — Betts described going through phases of anger, sadness, and eventual acceptance. Combined with the death of his stepfather during the season, the toll was heavy both physically and mentally.
injuries.personal_note: Betts described going through phases of anger, sadness, and eventual acceptance regarding the illness. Combined with the death of his stepfather during the season, the toll was heavy both physically and mentally. 'I'm OK if I don't play well, but I'm not OK with not playing well and not being who I am.'

injuries.status: Recovered — activated from IL in early June 2025 (missed ~32 games)
injuries.details: Strained right oblique, placed on 10-day IL. Manager Dave Roberts indicated 4-6 weeks for recovery, though he would 'take the under' on the timeline. Betts went on a rehab assignment with Triple-A Oklahoma City, making two rehab starts. Activated from IL ahead of a series and returned to the Dodgers lineup batting second vs the Giants. Missed approximately 32 games total.

injuries.status: Recovered — returned to lineup June 3, 2025 after 4-game absence
injuries.details: Fractured the tip of the fourth left toe by bumping into a wall while trying to make his way to the bathroom in the dark during a road trip. Could not wear closed-toed shoes initially. Broke in late May/early June 2025. Did light on-field work over the weekend but still managing the fracture. Returned to the Dodgers lineup as the 2-hole and shortstop in the series against the New York Mets on June 3, 2025.

Mookie Betts Returns To Dodgers Lineup With Broken Toe Still On The Mend

injuries.details: Removed in the first inning of a game versus the Washington Nationals on August 4, 2025, due to lower back pain. Walked to first base and scored on Freeman's double, but was replaced by Miguel Rojas at shortstop when the Dodgers took the field. Expected to be evaluated on a game-by-game basis. By August, Betts was hitting .330/.405/.454 since Aug. 5, showing signs of his signature form returning.

injuries.status: Active — playing through daily maintenance regimen
injuries.details: Sprained right ankle in the final home series of the 2024 regular season (Sept 25) against the Tigers; missed the last 3 regular-season games but played through the injury during the entire 2024 postseason, including the World Series where he hit a walk-off grand slam in Game 1 and earned World Series MVP honors. Underwent surgery in early December 2024 — debridement and removal of loose bodies from the right ankle. Surgeons also noted torn ligaments but those had healed enough they didn't need surgical repair. Freeman described it as 'quite a nasty injury.' Used a boost treadmill (body-weight de-loading) during early rehab. Prior to surgery, played through the sprain during NLDS and NLCS before landing on the IL for the postseason.
injuries.rehab_note: Used a boost treadmill (body-weight de-loading) during early rehab. Prior to surgery, played through the sprain during NLDS and NLCS before landing on the IL for the postseason.

Freddie Freeman Provides Massive Injury Update, Timeline to Return to Dodgers

injuries.details: Torn rib muscle and broken rib cartilage from the 2024 postseason. While the rib healed, scar tissue buildup in the area caused Freeman to miss the Dodgers' Tokyo Series opener in mid-March 2025. He continued to manage the issue through the early 2025 season.
injuries.rehab_note: Scar tissue buildup in the rib limited his mobility early in 2025. The rib cartilage damage from the 2024 postseason was fought through to earn World Series MVP.

sources.title: Freddie Freeman status for 2025 Opening Day

injuries.status: Recovered from acute episode — now in long-term maintenance
injuries.details: Slipped in the shower at home on March 31, 2025 — described as a 'freak accident' — and reaggravated his surgically repaired right ankle. Placed on 10-day IL (retroactive to March 31, eligible to return April 10). MRI revealed fraying of scar tissue. Activated around April 10, 2025, and hit .376 with a 1.171 OPS through 30 games at mid-season — 40 points above his previous career best through 30 games. Freeman reported feeling 'the best I've felt since I got hurt.' Undergoes approximately 1.5 hours of daily ankle treatment before and after every game, uses heel lifts and tape. Manager Dave Roberts: 'He'll be the first to tell you his time on the IL was beneficial.' Freeman is expected to continue treatment until at least the All-Star break but is not 100%.

sources.title: Freddie Freeman returns to Dodgers' lineup after missing 9 games with ankle injury from shower slip
Freddie Freeman having strong season despite ankle issues
Los Angeles Dodgers Shares New Freddie Freeman Injury Return Timeline

injuries.details: Left wrist contusion from being hit by an 88 mph sinker from Jose Quintana on July 20, 2025. X-rays negative. Described as day-to-day by manager Dave Roberts: 'Dodged a bullet.' If Freeman were forced to miss time, Dalton Rushing would start at first base.

sources.title: Dodgers' Dave Roberts Provides Freddie Freeman Injury Update

injuries.status: Active — returned quickly, no setbacks expected
injuries.details: Exited a game on August 27, 2025 with a neck stinger radiating into his right shoulder. Returned to the lineup two days later. Manager Dave Roberts: 'There's still some kind of lingering effects, but nothing compromising.' Dodgers used the off day to keep him comfortable. Not expected to miss significant time. Freeman continues his daily 1.5-hour maintenance regimen for his surgically repaired ankle.
